subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content: Monster boards (one per monster, each with Life Point and Victory Point dials), matching monster cardboard figures, 1 Tokyo board (Tokyo City and Tokyo Bay), black dice (6 used each turn), 2 green dice, Power card deck, Energy cubes, and 28 tokens total: 3 Smoke, 1 Mimic, 12 Shrink, 12 Poison.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content: 1) Place the Tokyo board at the center of the table. 2) Shuffle all Power cards to form a face-down deck. 3) Reveal the top 3 Power cards face up beside Tokyo as the market. 4) Place the 28 tokens nearby. 5) Put the 6 black dice in the center. 6) Keep the 2 green dice set aside (only used when card effects allow). 7) Make a common pool of all Energy cubes. 8) Choose board zones by player count: with 2-4 players, use Tokyo City only; with 5-6 players, use both Tokyo City and Tokyo Bay.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content: Each player chooses a Monster and takes the matching Monster board and cardboard figure. Set that Monster's Life Points to 10 and Victory Points to 0. Keep your figure near your board until you enter Tokyo, then place it in Tokyo City or Tokyo Bay as required.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content: All players roll the 6 black dice to determine first player. The player with the most Claw results starts; ties are rerolled among tied players until one player has the most. No Monster begins in Tokyo. The first player takes the first full turn and will be required to enter Tokyo during that turn's Enter Tokyo step if Tokyo is empty.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Turns proceed clockwise. On your turn, follow these 5 steps in order:
1. Roll Dice
   - You may roll up to three times total.
   - First roll: roll all 6 black dice (plus 1-2 green dice only if a Power card says you can).
   - Before second and third rolls, you may keep any dice and reroll the rest.
   - On the third roll, you may reroll dice you had previously set aside if desired.

2. Resolve Dice
   - Resolve all symbols shown on your final results.
   - You may resolve in any order, but all dice must be resolved.

3. Enter Tokyo
   - If no one is in Tokyo, you must enter Tokyo City.
   - In 5-6 players, if Tokyo City is occupied and Tokyo Bay is empty, you must enter Tokyo Bay.

4. Buy Power Cards
   - You may buy one or more face-up Power cards by paying Energy equal to each card's cost.
   - Replace bought cards immediately from the deck; newly revealed cards are available immediately.
   - You may also pay 2 Energy to sweep (discard) all three face-up cards and reveal three new ones.
   - As long as you can pay, you may keep buying and/or sweeping in this step.

5. End of Turn
   - Resolve any effects that trigger at end of turn.
   - Pass the dice to the player on your left.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
Final dice results create your turn's actions:

A) Number symbols (1, 2, 3) -> Gain Victory Points
- If you roll three of a kind of a number, gain VP equal to that number.
- Each additional die showing the same number adds +1 VP.
  Example: four 2s = 2 VP +1 extra = 3 VP.

B) Energy symbols -> Gain Energy cubes
- Gain 1 Energy cube from pool for each Energy symbol rolled.
- Keep Energy in front of you until spent on cards/effects.

C) Claw symbols -> Deal damage
- Each Claw causes 1 Life loss to eligible target monsters.
- If you are in Tokyo (City or Bay), your Claws hit all monsters outside Tokyo.
- If you are outside Tokyo, your Claws hit all monsters in Tokyo (both City and Bay).
- Monsters in Tokyo do not Claw each other directly as part of this targeting rule.

D) Heart symbols -> Heal
- If you are outside Tokyo, gain 1 Life per Heart rolled.
- You cannot heal above 10 Life.
- If you are in Tokyo, Heart symbols do not heal you (unless a Power card effect says otherwise).

Damage, elimination, and discards
- A Monster that loses its last Life is eliminated immediately.
- Eliminated Monsters discard all Keep cards and all Energy cubes.

Yielding Tokyo
- A Monster in Tokyo may Yield only after losing Life from another Monster's Claw roll.
- That Monster still takes the damage first.
- Yield is not allowed from Life loss caused by Power card effects; card-effect damage is different from Claw damage for yielding purposes.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
Tokyo control rules and bonuses:
- Entering Tokyo grants +1 Victory Point immediately.
- Starting your turn in Tokyo grants +2 Victory Points.
- Monsters in Tokyo cannot use Heart dice for healing.

Tokyo zones (5-6 players):
- Tokyo consists of Tokyo City and Tokyo Bay. Both count as 'in Tokyo.'
- If Tokyo City is occupied and Tokyo Bay empty, entering monster must take Tokyo Bay.
- If player count drops to 4 or fewer, any Monster in Tokyo Bay must leave Tokyo Bay immediately, unless Tokyo City is empty (in that case it moves to Tokyo City).

Power card types:
- Keep cards: stay face up in front of you until game end unless an effect removes them.
- Discard cards: resolve immediately when bought, then discard.

Card market cycling:
- Buying cards and sweeping are both optional but repeatable in Step 4 as long as you can pay.
- Replacements happen immediately, which can create chain-buy turns if you have enough Energy.

Glossary-linked timing:
- Roll means a throw of selected dice; before each new roll, you choose which dice to keep/reroll.
- Yield occurs only when damaged by Claw dice from another Monster and happens before the attacker's Enter Tokyo step.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
End triggers (two possible win conditions):
1) Victory Point win: the first Monster to reach 20 Victory Points wins.
2) Elimination win: if only one Monster remains alive, that Monster wins immediately.

How Victory Points are gained:
- From number sets on dice (three of a kind of 1/2/3, plus +1 per extra matching die).
- +1 VP when entering Tokyo.
- +2 VP when starting your turn in Tokyo.
- Additional VP from Power card effects (when relevant).

Damage and survival path to victory:
- Claw damage can eliminate players; the last survivor wins even if no one reached 20 VP.

Ties and simultaneous outcomes:
- The provided rule text defines winner as the Monster who reaches 20 first or last survivor and does not provide a separate tie-break paragraph.
- Resolve effects in turn order and current-action sequence; if a unique winner still cannot be distinguished from the same resolution window, treat as shared victory by table agreement.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
1) Build early Energy income. In the first turns, prioritize at least some Energy faces so you can buy Power cards before the market's best tempo cards disappear.
2) Take Tokyo when you can survive it. Early +1 on entry and +2 on start snowballs points, but only if your Life buffer is healthy enough to absorb return fire.
3) Decide your lane by table state: if opponents are low Life, lean Claws; if table is healthy and passive, race VP with numbers plus Tokyo points.
4) Keep rerolls purpose-driven. New players often reroll everything; better play is to lock 2-4 dice toward one concrete objective each turn (VP set, Energy spike, or damage burst).
5) Watch 5-6 player zone pressure. In larger games, City/Bay occupancy changes damage exposure and entry obligations—plan your health and yields around both spaces, not just one.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
1) Staying in Tokyo with critically low Life. The +2 VP is tempting, but getting eliminated loses all future turns and discards your board state.
2) Ignoring Energy economy. Players who skip Energy early fall behind on card power and cannot pivot when dice are poor.
3) Chasing number sets every turn regardless of board state. Sometimes one Claw or one Heart matters more than finishing a low-value set.
4) Forgetting Hearts do nothing in Tokyo. Misplaying this rule causes preventable knockouts.
5) Yielding Tokyo at the wrong time. Yielding too early can give free VP tempo; yielding too late can get you eliminated. Learn to evaluate incoming threat from all active players.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
1) VP race vs knockout plan: Do you invest rolls into numbers/Tokyo scoring, or pressure Life totals with Claws to force eliminations?
2) Spend Energy now vs save for sweep/chains: Immediate buy can stabilize your turn, but banking Energy enables multi-buy turns and market control via repeated sweeps.
3) Hold Tokyo vs Yield Tokyo: Keeping Tokyo increases scoring speed but blocks Heart healing and paints a target on you. Yielding preserves Life and timing, but can hand tempo to the attacker.

metadata.notes: Generated from provided King of Tokyo rulebook excerpt; scoring/timing language aligned to supplied text.
